failedAssertions.reason: auth login validates key against /api/user/profile which returns 404 for API key auth. Profile route uses getRequestContext() instead of getResolvedContext(), resolving userId as __api_key_pending__.
metrics list crashes with 'metricTypes.map is not a function'. API returns {data:{types:[]}} but CLI does response.data.map() expecting array directly.
metrics list --output json crashes with same metricTypes.map bug as VAL-CLI-005.
metrics get returns empty data. Health-data route uses getRequestContext() — userId is __api_key_pending__ with API key auth so no data matches.
shares create sends wrong field names: metrics (API wants allowed_metrics), start_date (API wants data_start), end_date (API wants data_end). Returns 400 validation error.
connections sync returns 500. Sync route uses getRequestContext() instead of getResolvedContext().
preferences set returns 500. Metric-preferences PUT route uses getRequestContext(). List and delete work.
profile returns 404 'User not found'. Profile route uses getRequestContext() — userId __api_key_pending__ not in database.
Two bugs: (1) CLI sends GET but export API only handles POST (405). (2) Export route also uses getRequestContext().
metrics list --output csv crashes with same metricTypes.map bug as VAL-CLI-005.
metrics summary crashes iterating metricTypes. Same {data:{types:[]}} shape mismatch as VAL-CLI-005.

rootCauses.description: Multiple API routes use getRequestContext() instead of getResolvedContext() for API key auth. With API key auth, middleware sets userId='__api_key_pending__'. Only getResolvedContext() resolves the API key to the real userId. Affected routes: /api/user/profile (GET), /api/user/export (POST), /api/health-data (GET), /api/connections (GET sync), /api/metric-preferences (PUT).
rootCauses.suggestedFix: Change affected route handlers from getRequestContext(request) to await getResolvedContext(request). This resolves the API key to the real user by looking up the key in the database.

rootCauses.description: CLI metrics command expects /api/health-data/types to return an array in response.data, but the API returns {data: {types: []}}. CLI does response.data.map() which fails because response.data is {types:[]} not an array.
rootCauses.suggestedFix: In packages/cli/src/commands/metrics.ts, change `const metricTypes = response.data;` to `const metricTypes = (response.data as any).types ?? response.data;` or update the API client generic type.

rootCauses.description: CLI shares create sends wrong field names to API: 'metrics' instead of 'allowed_metrics', 'start_date' instead of 'data_start', 'end_date' instead of 'data_end'.
rootCauses.suggestedFix: In packages/cli/src/commands/shares.ts, update the POST body to use API field names: allowed_metrics, data_start, data_end.

rootCauses.description: CLI export command sends GET to /api/user/export but the API route only defines a POST handler.
rootCauses.suggestedFix: Change export command from client.get() to client.post() in packages/cli/src/commands/export.ts.

appliedUpdates.description: Added Flow Validator Guidance: CLI section with test accounts, API keys, isolation rules, running commands guide, and known CLI issues discovered during testing.
